NKJV.1: Now a certain man was sick, Lazarus of Bethany, the town of Mary and her sister Martha.
NKJV.2: It was that Mary who anointed the Lord with fragrant oil and wiped His feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was sick.
NKJV.3: Therefore the sisters sent to Him, saying, “Lord, behold, he whom You love is sick.”
NKJV.4: When Jesus heard that, He said, “This sickness is not unto death, but for the glory of God, that the Son of God may be glorified through it.”
NKJV.5: Now Jesus loved Martha and her sister and Lazarus.
NKJV.6: So, when He heard that he was sick, He stayed two more days in the place where He was.
NKJV.7: Then after this He said to the disciples, “Let us go to Judea again.”
NKJV.8: The disciples said to Him, “Rabbi, lately the Jews sought to stone You, and are You going there again?”
NKJV.9: Jesus answered, “Are there not twelve hours in the day?  If anyone walks in the day, he does not stumble, because he sees the  light of this world.
NKJV.10: But  if one walks in the night, he stumbles, because the light is not in him.”
NKJV.11: These things He said, and after that He said to them, “Our friend Lazarus  sleeps, but I go that I may wake him up.”
NKJV.12: Then His disciples said, “Lord, if he sleeps he will get well.”
NKJV.13: However, Jesus spoke of his death, but they thought that He was speaking about taking rest in sleep.
NKJV.14: Then Jesus said to them plainly, “Lazarus is dead.
NKJV.15: And I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, that you may believe. Nevertheless let us go to him.”
NKJV.16: Then Thomas, who is called the Twin, said to his fellow disciples, “Let us also go, that we may die with Him.”
NKJV.17: So when Jesus came, He found that he had already been in the tomb four days.
NKJV.18: Now Bethany was near Jerusalem, about two miles away.
NKJV.19: And many of the Jews had joined the women around Martha and Mary, to comfort them concerning their brother.
NKJV.20: Now Martha, as soon as she heard that Jesus was coming, went and met Him, but Mary was sitting in the house.
NKJV.21: Now Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, if You had been here, my brother would not have died.
NKJV.22: But even now I know that whatever You ask of God, God will give You.”
NKJV.23: Jesus said to her, “Your brother will rise again.”
NKJV.24: Martha said to Him, “I know that he will rise again in the resurrection at the last day.”
NKJV.25: Jesus said to her, “I am  the resurrection and the life.  He who believes in Me, though he may  die, he shall live.
NKJV.26: And whoever lives and believes in Me shall never die. Do you believe this?”
NKJV.27: She said to Him, “Yes, Lord, I believe that You are the Christ, the Son of God, who is to come into the world.”
NKJV.28: And when she had said these things, she went her way and secretly called Mary her sister, saying, “The Teacher has come and is calling for you.”
NKJV.29: As soon as she heard that, she arose quickly and came to Him.
NKJV.30: Now Jesus had not yet come into the town, but was in the place where Martha met Him.
NKJV.31: Then the Jews who were with her in the house, and comforting her, when they saw that Mary rose up quickly and went out, followed her, saying, “She is going to the tomb to weep there.”
NKJV.32: Then, when Mary came where Jesus was, and saw Him, she fell down at His feet, saying to Him, “Lord, if You had been here, my brother would not have died.”
NKJV.33: Therefore, when Jesus saw her weeping, and the Jews who came with her weeping, He groaned in the spirit and was troubled.
NKJV.34: And He said, “Where have you laid him?” They said to Him, “Lord, come and see.”
NKJV.36: Then the Jews said, “See how He loved him!”
NKJV.37: And some of them said, “Could not this Man, who opened the eyes of the blind, also have kept this man from dying?”
NKJV.38: Then Jesus, again groaning in Himself, came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone lay against it.
NKJV.39: Jesus said, “Take away the stone.” Martha, the sister of him who was dead, said to Him, “Lord, by this time there is a stench, for he has been dead four days.”
NKJV.40: Jesus said to her, “Did I not say to you that if you would believe you would  see the glory of God?”
NKJV.41: Then they took away the stone from the place where the dead man was lying. And Jesus lifted up His eyes and said, “Father, I thank You that You have heard Me.
NKJV.42: And I know that You always hear Me, but because of the people who are standing by I said this, that they may believe that You sent Me.”
NKJV.43: Now when He had said these things, He cried with a loud voice, “Lazarus, come forth!”
NKJV.44: And he who had died came out bound hand and foot with graveclothes, and his face was wrapped with a cloth. Jesus said to them, “Loose him, and let him go.”
NKJV.45: Then many of the Jews who had come to Mary, and had seen the things Jesus did, believed in Him.
NKJV.46: But some of them went away to the Pharisees and told them the things Jesus did.
NKJV.47: Then the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered a council and said, “What shall we do? For this Man works many signs.
NKJV.48: If we let Him alone like this, everyone will believe in Him, and the Romans will come and take away both our place and nation.”
NKJV.49: And one of them, Caiaphas, being high priest that year, said to them, “You know nothing at all,
NKJV.50: nor do you consider that it is expedient for us that one man should die for the people, and not that the whole nation should perish.”
NKJV.51: Now this he did not say on his own authority; but being high priest that year he prophesied that Jesus would die for the nation,
NKJV.52: and not for that nation only, but also that He would gather together in one the children of God who were scattered abroad.
NKJV.53: Then, from that day on, they plotted to put Him to death.
NKJV.54: Therefore Jesus no longer walked openly among the Jews, but went from there into the country near the wilderness, to a city called Ephraim, and there remained with His disciples.
NKJV.55: And the Passover of the Jews was near, and many went from the country up to Jerusalem before the Passover, to purify themselves.
NKJV.56: Then they sought Jesus, and spoke among themselves as they stood in the temple, “What do you think—that He will not come to the feast?”
NKJV.57: Now both the chief priests and the Pharisees had given a command, that if anyone knew where He was, he should report it, that they might seize Him.

ESV.1: Now a certain man was ill, Lazarus of Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha.
ESV.2: It was Mary who anointed the Lord with ointment and wiped his feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was ill.
ESV.3: So the sisters sent to him, saying, “Lord, he whom you love is ill.”
ESV.4: But when Jesus heard it he said,  “This illness does not lead to death. It is for the glory of God, so that the Son of God may be glorified through it.”
ESV.5: Now Jesus loved Martha and her sister and Lazarus.
ESV.6: So, when he heard that Lazarus  was ill, he stayed two days longer in the place where he was.
ESV.7: Then after this he said to the disciples,  “Let us go to Judea again.”
ESV.8: The disciples said to him, “Rabbi, the Jews were just now seeking to stone you, and are you going there again?”
ESV.9: Jesus answered,  “Are there not twelve hours in the day? If anyone walks in the day, he does not stumble, because he sees the light of this world.
ESV.10: But if anyone walks in the night, he stumbles, because the light is not in him.”
ESV.11: After saying these things, he said to them, “Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep, but I go to awaken him.”
ESV.12: The disciples said to him, “Lord, if he has fallen asleep, he will recover.”
ESV.13: Now Jesus had spoken of his death, but they thought that he meant taking rest in sleep.
ESV.14: Then Jesus told them plainly, “Lazarus has died,
ESV.15: and for your sake I am glad that I was not there, so that you may believe. But let us go to him.”
ESV.16: So Thomas, called the Twin,  said to his fellow disciples, “Let us also go, that we may die with him.”
ESV.17: Now when Jesus came, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb four days.
ESV.18: Bethany was near Jerusalem, about two miles  off,
ESV.19: and many of the Jews had come to Martha and Mary to console them concerning their brother.
ESV.20: So when Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went and met him, but Mary remained seated in the house.
ESV.21: Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.
ESV.22: But even now I know that whatever you ask from God, God will give you.”
ESV.23: Jesus said to her, “Your brother will rise again.”
ESV.24: Martha said to him, “I know that he will rise again in the resurrection on the last day.”
ESV.25: Jesus said to her,  “I am the resurrection and the life.  Whoever believes in me, though he die, yet shall he live,
ESV.26: and everyone who lives and believes in me shall never die. Do you believe this?”
ESV.27: She said to him, “Yes, Lord; I believe that you are the Christ, the Son of God, who is coming into the world.”
ESV.28: When she had said this, she went and called her sister Mary, saying in private, “The Teacher is here and is calling for you.”
ESV.29: And when she heard it, she rose quickly and went to him.
ESV.30: Now Jesus had not yet come into the village, but was still in the place where Martha had met him.
ESV.31: When the Jews who were with her in the house, consoling her, saw Mary rise quickly and go out, they followed her, supposing that she was going to the tomb to weep there.
ESV.32: Now when Mary came to where Jesus was and saw him, she fell at his feet, saying to him, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.”
ESV.33: When Jesus saw her weeping, and the Jews who had come with her also weeping, he was deeply moved  in his spirit and greatly troubled.
ESV.34: And he said, “Where have you laid him?” They said to him, “Lord, come and see.”
ESV.36: So the Jews said, “See how he loved him!”
ESV.37: But some of them said, “Could not he who opened the eyes of the blind man also have kept this man from dying?”
ESV.38: Then Jesus, deeply moved again, came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone lay against it.
ESV.39: Jesus said, “Take away the stone.” Martha, the sister of the dead man, said to him, “Lord, by this time there will be an odor, for he has been dead four days.”
ESV.40: Jesus said to her,  “Did I not tell you that if you believed you would see the glory of God?”
ESV.41: So they took away the stone. And Jesus lifted up his eyes and said, “Father, I thank you that you have heard me.
ESV.42: I knew that you always hear me, but I said this on account of the people standing around, that they may believe that you sent me.”
ESV.43: When he had said these things, he cried out with a loud voice, “Lazarus, come out.”
ESV.44: The man who had died came out, his hands and feet bound with linen strips, and his face wrapped with a cloth. Jesus said to them, “Unbind him, and let him go.”
ESV.45: Many of the Jews therefore, who had come with Mary and had seen what he did, believed in him,
ESV.46: but some of them went to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.
ESV.47: So the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered the council and said, “What are we to do? For this man performs many signs.
ESV.48: If we let him go on like this, everyone will believe in him, and the Romans will come and take away both our place and our nation.”
ESV.49: But one of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, said to them, “You know nothing at all.
ESV.50: Nor do you understand that it is better for you that one man should die for the people, not that the whole nation should perish.”
ESV.51: He did not say this of his own accord, but being high priest that year he prophesied that Jesus would die for the nation,
ESV.52: and not for the nation only, but also to gather into one the children of God who are scattered abroad.
ESV.53: So from that day on they made plans to put him to death.
ESV.54: Jesus therefore no longer walked openly among the Jews, but went from there to the region near the wilderness, to a town called Ephraim, and there he stayed with the disciples.
ESV.55: Now the Passover of the Jews was at hand, and many went up from the country to Jerusalem before the Passover to purify themselves.
ESV.56: They were looking for  Jesus and saying to one another as they stood in the temple, “What do you think? That he will not come to the feast at all?”
ESV.57: Now the chief priests and the Pharisees had given orders that if anyone knew where he was, he should let them know, so that they might arrest him.

NASB.1: Now a certain man was sick, Lazarus of Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha.
NASB.2: It was the Mary who anointed the Lord with ointment, and wiped His feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was sick.
NASB.3: So the sisters sent [word] to Him, saying, 'Lord, behold, he whom You love is sick.'
NASB.4: But when Jesus heard [this], He said, 'This sickness is not to end in death, but for the glory of God, so that the Son of God may be glorified by it.'
NASB.5: Now Jesus loved Martha and her sister and Lazarus.
NASB.6: So when He heard that he was sick, He then stayed two days [longer] in the place where He was.
NASB.7: Then after this He said to the disciples, 'Let us go to Judea again.'
NASB.8: The disciples said to Him, 'Rabbi, the Jews were just now seeking to stone You, and are You going there again?'
NASB.9: Jesus answered, 'Are there not twelve hours in the day? If anyone walks in the day, he does not stumble, because he sees the light of this world.
NASB.10: 'But if anyone walks in the night, he stumbles, because the light is not in him.'
NASB.11: This He said, and after that He said to them, 'Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep; but I go, so that I may awaken him out of sleep.'
NASB.12: The disciples then said to Him, 'Lord, if he has fallen asleep, he will recover.'
NASB.13: Now Jesus had spoken of his death, but they thought that He was speaking of literal sleep.
NASB.14: So Jesus then said to them plainly, 'Lazarus is dead,
NASB.15: and I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, so that you may believe; but let us go to him.'
NASB.16: Therefore Thomas, who is called Didymus, said to [his] fellow disciples, 'Let us also go, so that we may die with Him.'
NASB.17: So when Jesus came, He found that he had already been in the tomb four days.
NASB.18: Now Bethany was near Jerusalem, about two miles off;
NASB.19: and many of the Jews had come to Martha and Mary, to console them concerning [their] brother.
NASB.20: Martha therefore, when she heard that Jesus was coming, went to meet Him, but Mary stayed at the house.
NASB.21: Martha then said to Jesus, 'Lord, if You had been here, my brother would not have died.
NASB.22: 'Even now I know that whatever You ask of God, God will give You.'
NASB.23: Jesus said to her, 'Your brother will rise again.'
NASB.24: Martha said to Him, 'I know that he will rise again in the resurrection on the last day.'
NASB.25: Jesus said to her, 'I am the resurrection and the life; he who believes in Me will live even if he dies,
NASB.26: and everyone who lives and believes in Me will never die. Do you believe this?'
NASB.27: She said to Him, 'Yes, Lord; I have believed that You are the Christ, the Son of God, [even] He who comes into the world.'
NASB.28: When she had said this, she went away and called Mary her sister, saying secretly, 'The Teacher is here and is calling for you.'
NASB.29: And when she heard it, she got up quickly and was coming to Him.
NASB.30: Now Jesus had not yet come into the village, but was still in the place where Martha met Him.
NASB.31: Then the Jews who were with her in the house, and consoling her, when they saw that Mary got up quickly and went out, they followed her, supposing that she was going to the tomb to weep there.
NASB.32: Therefore, when Mary came where Jesus was, she saw Him, and fell at His feet, saying to Him, 'Lord, if You had been here, my brother would not have died.'
NASB.33: When Jesus therefore saw her weeping, and the Jews who came with her [also] weeping, He was deeply moved in spirit and was troubled,
NASB.34: and said, 'Where have you laid him?' They said to Him, 'Lord, come and see.'
NASB.36: So the Jews were saying, 'See how He loved him!'
NASB.37: But some of them said, 'Could not this man, who opened the eyes of the blind man, have kept this man also from dying?'
NASB.38: So Jesus, again being deeply moved within, came to the tomb. Now it was a cave, and a stone was lying against it.
NASB.39: Jesus said, 'Remove the stone.' Martha, the sister of the deceased, said to Him, 'Lord, by this time there will be a stench, for he has been [dead] four days.'
NASB.40: Jesus said to her, 'Did I not say to you that if you believe, you will see the glory of God?'
NASB.41: So they removed the stone. Then Jesus raised His eyes, and said, 'Father, I thank You that You have heard Me.
NASB.42: 'I knew that You always hear Me; but because of the people standing around I said it, so that they may believe that You sent Me.'
NASB.43: When He had said these things, He cried out with a loud voice, 'Lazarus, come forth.'
NASB.44: The man who had died came forth, bound hand and foot with wrappings, and his face was wrapped around with a cloth. Jesus said to them, 'Unbind him, and let him go.'
NASB.45: Therefore many of the Jews who came to Mary, and saw what He had done, believed in Him.
NASB.46: But some of them went to the Pharisees and told them the things which Jesus had done.
NASB.47: Therefore the chief priests and the Pharisees convened a council, and were saying, 'What are we doing? For this man is performing many signs.
NASB.48: 'If we let Him [go on] like this, all men will believe in Him, and the Romans will come and take away both our place and our nation.'
NASB.49: But one of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, said to them, 'You know nothing at all,
NASB.50: nor do you take into account that it is expedient for you that one man die for the people, and that the whole nation not perish.'
NASB.51: Now he did not say this on his own initiative, but being high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus was going to die for the nation,
NASB.52: and not for the nation only, but in order that He might also gather together into one the children of God who are scattered abroad.
NASB.53: So from that day on they planned together to kill Him.
NASB.54: Therefore Jesus no longer continued to walk publicly among the Jews, but went away from there to the country near the wilderness, into a city called Ephraim; and there He stayed with the disciples.
NASB.55: Now the Passover of the Jews was near, and many went up to Jerusalem out of the country before the Passover to purify themselves.
NASB.56: So they were seeking for Jesus, and were saying to one another as they stood in the temple, 'What do you think; that He will not come to the feast at all?'
NASB.57: Now the chief priests and the Pharisees had given orders that if anyone knew where He was, he was to report it, so that they might seize Him.

NLT.1: A man named Lazarus was sick. He lived in Bethany with his sisters, Mary and Martha.
NLT.2: This is the Mary who later poured the expensive perfume on the Lord’s feet and wiped them with her hair. Her brother, Lazarus, was sick.
NLT.3: So the two sisters sent a message to Jesus telling him, “Lord, your dear friend is very sick.”
NLT.4: But when Jesus heard about it he said, “Lazarus’s sickness will not end in death. No, it happened for the glory of God so that the Son of God will receive glory from this.”
NLT.5: So although Jesus loved Martha, Mary, and Lazarus,
NLT.6: he stayed where he was for the next two days.
NLT.7: Finally, he said to his disciples, “Let’s go back to Judea.”
NLT.8: But his disciples objected. “Rabbi,” they said, “only a few days ago the people in Judea were trying to stone you. Are you going there again?”
NLT.9: Jesus replied, “There are twelve hours of daylight every day. During the day people can walk safely. They can see because they have the light of this world.
NLT.10: But at night there is danger of stumbling because they have no light.”
NLT.11: Then he said, “Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep, but now I will go and wake him up.”
NLT.12: The disciples said, “Lord, if he is sleeping, he will soon get better!”
NLT.13: They thought Jesus meant Lazarus was simply sleeping, but Jesus meant Lazarus had died.
NLT.14: So he told them plainly, “Lazarus is dead.
NLT.15: And for your sakes, I’m glad I wasn’t there, for now you will really believe. Come, let’s go see him.”
NLT.16: Thomas, nicknamed the Twin, said to his fellow disciples, “Let’s go, too — and die with Jesus.”
NLT.17: When Jesus arrived at Bethany, he was told that Lazarus had already been in his grave for four days.
NLT.18: Bethany was only a few miles down the road from Jerusalem,
NLT.19: and many of the people had come to console Martha and Mary in their loss.
NLT.20: When Martha got word that Jesus was coming, she went to meet him. But Mary stayed in the house.
NLT.21: Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, if only you had been here, my brother would not have died.
NLT.22: But even now I know that God will give you whatever you ask.”
NLT.23: Jesus told her, “Your brother will rise again.”
NLT.24: “Yes,” Martha said, “he will rise when everyone else rises, at the last day.”
NLT.25: Jesus told her, “I am the resurrection and the life. Anyone who believes in me will live, even after dying.
NLT.26: Everyone who lives in me and believes in me will never ever die. Do you believe this, Martha?”
NLT.27: “Yes, Lord,” she told him. “I have always believed you are the Messiah, the Son of God, the one who has come into the world from God.”
NLT.28: Then she returned to Mary. She called Mary aside from the mourners and told her, “The Teacher is here and wants to see you.”
NLT.29: So Mary immediately went to him.
NLT.30: Jesus had stayed outside the village, at the place where Martha met him.
NLT.31: When the people who were at the house consoling Mary saw her leave so hastily, they assumed she was going to Lazarus’s grave to weep. So they followed her there.
NLT.32: When Mary arrived and saw Jesus, she fell at his feet and said, “Lord, if only you had been here, my brother would not have died.”
NLT.33: When Jesus saw her weeping and saw the other people wailing with her, a deep anger welled up within him, and he was deeply troubled.
NLT.34: “Where have you put him?” he asked them. They told him, “Lord, come and see.”
NLT.36: The people who were standing nearby said, “See how much he loved him!”
NLT.37: But some said, “This man healed a blind man. Couldn’t he have kept Lazarus from dying?”
NLT.38: Jesus was still angry as he arrived at the tomb, a cave with a stone rolled across its entrance.
NLT.39: “Roll the stone aside,” Jesus told them. But Martha, the dead man’s sister, protested, “Lord, he has been dead for four days. The smell will be terrible.”
NLT.40: Jesus responded, “Didn’t I tell you that you would see God’s glory if you believe?”
NLT.41: So they rolled the stone aside. Then Jesus looked up to heaven and said, “Father, thank you for hearing me.
NLT.42: You always hear me, but I said it out loud for the sake of all these people standing here, so that they will believe you sent me.”
NLT.44: And the dead man came out, his hands and feet bound in graveclothes, his face wrapped in a headcloth. Jesus told them, “Unwrap him and let him go!”
NLT.45: Many of the people who were with Mary believed in Jesus when they saw this happen.
NLT.46: But some went to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.
NLT.47: Then the leading priests and Pharisees called the high council together. “What are we going to do?” they asked each other. “This man certainly performs many miraculous signs.
NLT.48: If we allow him to go on like this, soon everyone will believe in him. Then the Roman army will come and destroy both our Temple and our nation.”
NLT.49: Caiaphas, who was high priest at that time, said, “You don’t know what you’re talking about!
NLT.50: You don’t realize that it’s better for you that one man should die for the people than for the whole nation to be destroyed.”
NLT.51: He did not say this on his own; as high priest at that time he was led to prophesy that Jesus would die for the entire nation.
NLT.52: And not only for that nation, but to bring together and unite all the children of God scattered around the world.
NLT.53: So from that time on, the Jewish leaders began to plot Jesus’ death.
NLT.54: As a result, Jesus stopped his public ministry among the people and left Jerusalem. He went to a place near the wilderness, to the village of Ephraim, and stayed there with his disciples.
NLT.55: It was now almost time for the Jewish Passover celebration, and many people from all over the country arrived in Jerusalem several days early so they could go through the purification ceremony before Passover began.
NLT.56: They kept looking for Jesus, but as they stood around in the Temple, they said to each other, “What do you think? He won’t come for Passover, will he?”
NLT.57: Meanwhile, the leading priests and Pharisees had publicly ordered that anyone seeing Jesus must report it immediately so they could arrest him.

WEB.1: Now a certain man was sick, Lazarus from Bethany, of the village of Mary and her sister, Martha.
WEB.2: It was that Mary who had anointed the Lord with ointment, and wiped his feet with her hair, whose brother, Lazarus, was sick.
WEB.3: The sisters therefore sent to him, saying, “Lord, behold, he for whom you have great affection is sick.”
WEB.4: But when Jesus heard it, he said, “This sickness is not to death, but for the glory of God, that God’s Son may be glorified by it.”
WEB.5: Now Jesus loved Martha, and her sister, and Lazarus.
WEB.6: When therefore he heard that he was sick, he stayed two days in the place where he was.
WEB.7: Then after this he said to the disciples, “Let’s go into Judea again.”
WEB.8: The disciples told him, “Rabbi, the Jews were just trying to stone you, and are you going there again?”
WEB.9: Jesus answered, “Aren’t there twelve hours of daylight? If a man walks in the day, he doesn’t stumble, because he sees the light of this world.
WEB.10: But if a man walks in the night, he stumbles, because the light isn’t in him.”
WEB.11: He said these things, and after that, he said to them, “Our friend, Lazarus, has fallen asleep, but I am going so that I may awake him out of sleep.”
WEB.12: The disciples therefore said, “Lord, if he has fallen asleep, he will recover.”
WEB.13: Now Jesus had spoken of his death, but they thought that he spoke of taking rest in sleep.
WEB.14: So Jesus said to them plainly then, “Lazarus is dead.
WEB.15: I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, so that you may believe. Nevertheless, let’s go to him.”
WEB.16: Thomas therefore, who is called Didymus,  said to his fellow disciples, “Let’s go also, that we may die with him.”
WEB.17: So when Jesus came, he found that he had been in the tomb four days already.
WEB.18: Now Bethany was near Jerusalem, about fifteen stadia  away.
WEB.19: Many of the Jews had joined the women around Martha and Mary, to console them concerning their brother.
WEB.20: Then when Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went and met him, but Mary stayed in the house.
WEB.21: Therefore Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, if you would have been here, my brother wouldn’t have died.
WEB.22: Even now I know that, whatever you ask of God, God will give you.”
WEB.23: Jesus said to her, “Your brother will rise again.”
WEB.24: Martha said to him, “I know that he will rise again in the resurrection at the last day.”
WEB.25: Jesus said to her, “I am the resurrection and the life. He who believes in me will still live, even if he dies.
WEB.26: Whoever lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this?”
WEB.27: She said to him, “Yes, Lord. I have come to believe that you are the Christ, God’s Son, he who comes into the world.”
WEB.28: When she had said this, she went away, and called Mary, her sister, secretly, saying, “The Teacher is here, and is calling you.”
WEB.29: When she heard this, she arose quickly, and went to him.
WEB.30: Now Jesus had not yet come into the village, but was in the place where Martha met him.
WEB.31: Then the Jews who were with her in the house, and were consoling her, when they saw Mary, that she rose up quickly and went out, followed her, saying, “She is going to the tomb to weep there.”
WEB.32: Therefore when Mary came to where Jesus was, and saw him, she fell down at his feet, saying to him, “Lord, if you would have been here, my brother wouldn’t have died.”
WEB.33: When Jesus therefore saw her weeping, and the Jews weeping who came with her, he groaned in the spirit, and was troubled,
WEB.34: and said, “Where have you laid him?” They told him, “Lord, come and see.”
WEB.36: The Jews therefore said, “See how much affection he had for him!”
WEB.37: Some of them said, “Couldn’t this man, who opened the eyes of him who was blind, have also kept this man from dying?”
WEB.38: Jesus therefore, again groaning in himself, came to the tomb. Now it was a cave, and a stone lay against it.
WEB.39: Jesus said, “Take away the stone.” Martha, the sister of him who was dead, said to him, “Lord, by this time there is a stench, for he has been dead four days.”
WEB.40: Jesus said to her, “Didn’t I tell you that if you believed, you would see God’s glory?”
WEB.41: So they took away the stone from the place where the dead man was lying.  Jesus lifted up his eyes, and said, “Father, I thank you that you listened to me.
WEB.42: I know that you always listen to me, but because of the multitude that stands around I said this, that they may believe that you sent me.”
WEB.43: When he had said this, he cried with a loud voice, “Lazarus, come out!”
WEB.44: He who was dead came out, bound hand and foot with wrappings, and his face was wrapped around with a cloth. Jesus said to them, “Free him, and let him go.”
WEB.45: Therefore many of the Jews, who came to Mary and saw what Jesus did, believed in him.
WEB.46: But some of them went away to the Pharisees, and told them the things which Jesus had done.
WEB.47: The chief priests therefore and the Pharisees gathered a council, and said, “What are we doing? For this man does many signs.
WEB.48: If we leave him alone like this, everyone will believe in him, and the Romans will come and take away both our place and our nation.”
WEB.49: But a certain one of them, Caiaphas, being high priest that year, said to them, “You know nothing at all,
WEB.50: nor do you consider that it is advantageous for us that one man should die for the people, and that the whole nation not perish.”
WEB.51: Now he didn’t say this of himself, but being high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus would die for the nation,
WEB.52: and not for the nation only, but that he might also gather together into one the children of God who are scattered abroad.
WEB.53: So from that day forward they took counsel that they might put him to death.
WEB.54: Jesus therefore walked no more openly among the Jews, but departed from there into the country near the wilderness, to a city called Ephraim. He stayed there with his disciples.
WEB.55: Now the Passover of the Jews was at hand. Many went up from the country to Jerusalem before the Passover, to purify themselves.
WEB.56: Then they sought for Jesus and spoke one with another, as they stood in the temple, “What do you think — that he isn’t coming to the feast at all?”
WEB.57: Now the chief priests and the Pharisees had commanded that if anyone knew where he was, he should report it, that they might seize him.

CSB17.1: Now a man was sick, Lazarus from Bethany, <sup>ⓒ</sup> the village of Mary and her sister Martha. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.2: Mary was the one who anointed the Lord with perfume and wiped his feet with her hair, <sup>ⓔ</sup> and it was her brother Lazarus who was sick.
CSB17.3: So the sisters sent a message to him: “Lord, the one you love is sick.”
CSB17.4: When Jesus heard it, he said, “This sickness will not end in death but is for the glory of God, <sup>ⓕ</sup> so that the Son of God <sup>ⓖ</sup> may be glorified through it.”
CSB17.5: Now Jesus loved Martha, her sister, and Lazarus.
CSB17.6: So when he heard that he was sick, he stayed two more days in the place where he was.

CSB17.9: “Aren’t there twelve hours in a day?” Jesus answered. “If anyone walks during the day, he doesn’t stumble, because he sees the light of this world. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.10: But if anyone walks during the night, <sup>ⓛ</sup>he does stumble, because the light is not in him.”
CSB17.11: He said this, and then he told them, “Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep, <sup>ⓜ</sup>but I’m on my way to wake him up.”
CSB17.12: Then the disciples said to him, “Lord, if he has fallen asleep, he will get well.”
CSB17.13: Jesus, however, was speaking about his death, but they thought he was speaking about natural sleep.
CSB17.14: So Jesus then told them plainly, “Lazarus has died.
CSB17.15: I’m glad for you that I wasn’t there so that you may believe. But let’s go to him.”
CSB17.16: Then Thomas <sup>ⓝ</sup> (called “Twin” <sup>[1]</sup>) said to his fellow disciples, “Let’s go too so that we may die with him.”
CSB17.17: When Jesus arrived, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb <sup>ⓐ</sup> four days.
CSB17.18: Bethany was near Jerusalem <sup>ⓑ</sup> (less than two miles <sup>[2]</sup> away).
CSB17.19: Many of the Jews had come to Martha and Mary to comfort them about their brother.
CSB17.20: As soon as Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went to meet him, but Mary remained seated in the house.
CSB17.21: Then Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldn’t have died.
CSB17.22: Yet even now I know that whatever you ask from God, God will give you.”
CSB17.23: “Your brother will rise <sup>ⓒ</sup> again,” Jesus told her.
CSB17.24: Martha said to him, “I know that he will rise again in the resurrection at the last day.” <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.25: Jesus said to her, “I am <sup>ⓔ</sup>the resurrection and the life. The one who believes in me, <sup>ⓕ</sup>even if he dies, will live. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.26: Everyone who lives and believes in me will never die. <sup>ⓗ</sup>Do you believe this?”
CSB17.27: “Yes, Lord,” she told him, “I believe you are the Messiah, <sup>ⓘ</sup> the Son <sup>ⓙ</sup> of God, who comes into the world.” <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.28: Having said this, she went back and called her sister Mary, saying in private, “The Teacher is here and is calling for you.”
CSB17.29: As soon as Mary heard this, she got up quickly and went to him. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.30: Jesus had not yet come into the village but was still in the place where Martha had met him.
CSB17.31: The Jews who were with her in the house consoling her saw that Mary got up quickly and went out. They followed her, supposing that she was going to the tomb <sup>ⓜ</sup> to cry there.
CSB17.32: As soon as Mary came to where Jesus was and saw him, she fell at his feet <sup>ⓝ</sup> and told him, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died!”
CSB17.33: When Jesus saw her crying, and the Jews who had come with her crying, he was deeply moved <sup>[3]</sup> in his spirit <sup>ⓐ</sup> and troubled.
CSB17.34: “Where have you put him?” he asked. “Lord,” they told him, “come and see.”
CSB17.36: So the Jews said, “See how he loved <sup>ⓒ</sup> him!”
CSB17.37: But some of them said, “Couldn’t he who opened the blind man’s eyes <sup>ⓓ</sup> also have kept this man from dying?”
CSB17.38: Then Jesus, deeply moved again, came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone was lying against it. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.39: “Remove the stone,” Jesus said. Martha, the dead man’s sister, told him, “Lord, there is already a stench because he has been dead four days.”
CSB17.40: Jesus said to her, “Didn’t I tell you that if you believed you would see the glory <sup>ⓕ</sup>of God?” <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.41: So they removed the stone. Then Jesus raised his eyes and said, “Father, I thank <sup>ⓗ</sup>you that you heard me. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.42: I know that you always hear me, but because of the crowd standing here I said this, so that they may believe you sent <sup>ⓙ</sup>me.”
CSB17.43: After he said this, he shouted with a loud voice, “Lazarus, come out!”
CSB17.44: The dead man came out bound hand and foot with linen strips and with his face wrapped in a cloth. Jesus said to them, “Unwrap him and let him go.”
CSB17.45: Therefore, many of the Jews who came to Mary and saw what he did believed in him. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.46: But some of them went to the Pharisees <sup>ⓛ</sup> and told them what Jesus had done.
CSB17.47: So the chief priests and the Pharisees convened the Sanhedrin <sup>ⓜ</sup> and were saying, “What are we going to do since this man is doing many signs?
CSB17.48: If we let him go on like this, everyone will believe in him, and the Romans <sup>ⓝ</sup> will come and take away both our place and our nation.”
CSB17.49: One of them, Caiaphas, <sup>ⓐ</sup> who was high priest <sup>ⓑ</sup> that year, said to them, “You know nothing at all!
CSB17.50: You’re not considering that it is to your <sup>[4]</sup> advantage that one man should die for the people rather than the whole nation perish.” <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.51: He did not say this on his own, but being high priest that year he prophesied that Jesus was going to die <sup>ⓓ</sup> for the nation,
CSB17.52: and not for the nation only, but also to unite the scattered children <sup>ⓔ</sup> of God.
CSB17.53: So from that day on they plotted to kill him. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.54: Jesus therefore no longer walked openly <sup>ⓖ</sup> among the Jews but departed from there to the countryside near the wilderness, <sup>ⓗ</sup> to a town called Ephraim, and he stayed there with the disciples.
CSB17.55: Now the Jewish Passover <sup>ⓘ</sup> was near, and many went up to Jerusalem <sup>ⓙ</sup> from the country to purify themselves before the Passover.
CSB17.56: They were looking for Jesus and asking one another as they stood in the temple: <sup>ⓚ</sup> “What do you think? He won’t come to the festival, <sup>ⓛ</sup> will he?”
CSB17.57: The chief priests <sup>ⓜ</sup> and the Pharisees had given orders that if anyone knew where he was, he should report it so that they could arrest him.

AMP.1: Now a certain man named Lazarus was sick. He was from Bethany, the village where Mary and her sister Martha lived.
AMP.2: It was the Mary who anointed the Lord with perfume and wiped His feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was sick.
AMP.3: So the sisters sent word to Him, saying, "Lord, he [our brother and Your friend] whom You love is sick."
AMP.4: When Jesus heard this, He said, "This sickness will not end in death; but [on the contrary it is] for the glory and honor of God, so that the Son of God may be glorified by it."
AMP.5: Now Jesus loved and was concerned about Martha and her sister and Lazarus [and considered them dear friends].
AMP.6: So [even] when He heard that Lazarus was sick, He stayed in the same place two more days.
AMP.7: Then He said to His disciples, "Let us go back to Judea."
AMP.8: The disciples said to Him, "Rabbi (Teacher), the Jews were only recently going to stone You, and You are [thinking of] going back there again?"
AMP.9: Jesus answered, "Are there not twelve hours [of light] in the day? Anyone who walks in the daytime does not stumble, because he sees [by] the light of this world.
AMP.10: But if anyone walks in the night, he stumbles, because there is no light in him."
AMP.11: He said this, and after that said, "Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep; but I am going there to wake him."
AMP.12: The disciples answered, "Lord, if he has fallen asleep, he will recover."
AMP.13: However, Jesus had spoken of his death, but they thought that He was referring to natural sleep.
AMP.14: So then Jesus told them plainly, "Lazarus is dead.
AMP.15: And for your sake I am glad that I was not there, so that you may believe. But let us go to him."
AMP.16: Then Thomas, who was called Didymus (the twin), said to his fellow disciples, "Let us go too, that we may die with Him."
AMP.17: So when Jesus arrived, He found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb four days.
AMP.18: Bethany was near Jerusalem, about two miles away;
AMP.19: and many of the Jews had come to see Martha and Mary, to comfort them concerning [the loss of] their brother.
AMP.20: So when Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went to meet Him, while Mary remained sitting in the house.
AMP.21: Then Martha said to Jesus, "Lord, if You had been here, my brother would not have died.
AMP.22: Even now I know that whatever You ask of God, God will give to You."
AMP.23: Jesus told her, "Your brother will rise [from the dead]."
AMP.24: Martha replied, "I know that he will rise [from the dead] in the resurrection on the last day."
AMP.25: Jesus said to her, " I am the Resurrection and the Life. Whoever believes in (adheres to, trusts in, relies on) Me [as Savior] will live even if he dies;
AMP.26: and everyone who lives and believes in Me [as Savior] will never die. Do you believe this?"
AMP.27: She said to Him, "Yes, Lord; I have believed and continue to believe that You are the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed), the Son of God, He who was [destined and promised] to come into the world [and it is for You that the world has waited]."
AMP.28: After she had said this, she left and called her sister Mary, privately whispering [to her], "The Teacher is here and is asking for you."
AMP.29: And when she heard this, she got up quickly and went to Him.
AMP.30: Now Jesus had not yet entered the village, but was still at the place where Martha had met Him.
AMP.31: So when the Jews who were with her in the house comforting her, saw how quickly Mary got up and left, they followed her, assuming that she was going to the tomb to weep there.
AMP.32: When Mary came [to the place] where Jesus was and saw Him, she fell at His feet, saying to Him, "Lord, if You had been here, my brother would not have died."
AMP.33: When Jesus saw her sobbing, and the Jews who had come with her also sobbing, He was deeply moved in spirit [to the point of anger at the sorrow caused by death] and was troubled,
AMP.34: and said, "Where have you laid him?" They said, "Lord, come and see."
AMP.36: So the Jews were saying, "See how He loved him [as a close friend]!"
AMP.37: But some of them said, "Could not this Man, who opened the blind man's eyes, have kept this man from dying?"
AMP.38: So Jesus, again deeply moved within [to the point of anger], approached the tomb. It was a cave, and a boulder was lying against it [to cover the entrance].
AMP.39: Jesus said, "Take away the stone." Martha, the sister of the dead man, said to Him, "Lord, by this time there will be an offensive odor, for he has been dead four days! [It is hopeless!]"
AMP.40: Jesus said to her, "Did I not say to you that if you believe [in Me], you will see the glory of God [the expression of His excellence]?"
AMP.41: So they took away the stone. And Jesus raised His eyes [toward heaven] and said, "Father, I thank You that You have heard Me.
AMP.42: I knew that You always hear Me and listen to Me; but I have said this because of the people standing around, so that they may believe that You have sent Me [and that You have made Me Your representative]."
AMP.43: When He had said this, He shouted with a loud voice, "Lazarus, come out!"
AMP.44: Out came the man who had been dead, his hands and feet tightly wrapped in burial cloths (linen strips), and with a [burial] cloth wrapped around his face. Jesus said to them, "Unwrap him and release him."
AMP.45: So then, many of the Jews who had come to [be with] Mary and who were eyewitnesses to what Jesus had done, believed in Him.
AMP.46: But some of them went back to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.
AMP.47: So the chief priests and Pharisees convened a council [of the leaders in Israel], and said, "What are we doing? For this man performs many signs (attesting miracles).
AMP.48: If we let Him go on like this, everyone will believe in Him, and the Romans will come and take away both our [holy] place (the temple) and our nation."
AMP.49: But one of them, Caiaphas, who was the high priest that year [the year of Christ's crucifixion], said to them, "You know nothing at all!
AMP.50: Nor do you understand that it is expedient and politically advantageous for you that one man die for the people, and that the whole nation not perish."
AMP.51: Now he did not say this [simply] on his own initiative; but being the high priest that year, he [was unknowingly used by God and] prophesied that Jesus was going to die for the nation,
AMP.52: and not only for the nation, but also for the purpose of gathering together into one body the children of God who have been scattered abroad.
AMP.53: So from that day on they planned together to kill Him.
AMP.54: For that reason Jesus no longer walked openly among the Jews, but left there and went to the district that borders on the uninhabited wilderness, to a town called Ephraim; and He stayed there with the disciples.
AMP.55: Now the Passover of the Jews was approaching, and many from the country went up to Jerusalem before Passover to purify themselves [ceremonially, so that they would be able to participate in the feast].
AMP.56: So they were looking for Jesus as they stood in the temple [area], and saying among themselves, "What do you think? Will He not come to the feast at all?"
AMP.57: Now the chief priests and Pharisees had given orders that if anyone knew where He was, he was to report it so that they might arrest Him.

MSG.1: A man was sick, Lazarus of Bethany, the town of Mary and her sister Martha.
MSG.2: This was the same Mary who massaged the Lord's feet with aromatic oils and then wiped them with her hair. It was her brother Lazarus who was sick.
MSG.3: So the sisters sent word to Jesus, "Master, the one you love so very much is sick."
MSG.4: When Jesus got the message, he said, "This sickness is not fatal. It will become an occasion to show God's glory by glorifying God's Son."
MSG.5: Jesus loved Martha and her sister and Lazarus,
MSG.6: but oddly, when he heard that Lazarus was sick, he stayed on where he was for two more days.
MSG.7: After the two days, he said to his disciples, "Let's go back to Judea."
MSG.8: They said, "Rabbi, you can't do that. The Jews are out to kill you, and you're going back?"
MSG.9: Jesus replied, "Are there not twelve hours of daylight? Anyone who walks in daylight doesn't stumble because there's plenty of light from the sun.
MSG.10: Walking at night, he might very well stumble because he can't see where he's going."
MSG.11: He said these things, and then announced, "Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep. I'm going to wake him up."
MSG.12: The disciples said, "Master, if he's gone to sleep, he'll get a good rest and wake up feeling fine."
MSG.13: Jesus was talking about death, while his disciples thought he was talking about taking a nap.
MSG.15: And I am glad for your sakes that I wasn't there. You're about to be given new grounds for believing. Now let's go to him."
MSG.16: That's when Thomas, the one called the Twin, said to his companions, "Come along. We might as well die with him."
MSG.17: When Jesus finally got there, he found Lazarus already four days dead.
MSG.18: Bethany was near Jerusalem, only a couple of miles away,
MSG.19: and many of the Jews were visiting Martha and Mary, sympathizing with them over their brother.
MSG.20: Martha heard Jesus was coming and went out to meet him. Mary remained in the house.
MSG.21: Martha said, "Master, if you'd been here, my brother wouldn't have died.
MSG.22: Even now, I know that whatever you ask God he will give you."
MSG.23: Jesus said, "Your brother will be raised up."
MSG.24: Martha replied, "I know that he will be raised up in the resurrection at the end of time."
MSG.25: "You don't have to wait for the End. I am, right now, Resurrection and Life. The one who believes in me, even though he or she dies, will live.
MSG.26: And everyone who lives believing in me does not ultimately die at all. Do you believe this?"
MSG.27: "Yes, Master. All along I have believed that you are the Messiah, the Son of God who comes into the world."
MSG.28: After saying this, she went to her sister Mary and whispered in her ear, "The Teacher is here and is asking for you."
MSG.29: The moment she heard that, she jumped up and ran out to him.
MSG.30: Jesus had not yet entered the town but was still at the place where Martha had met him.
MSG.31: When her sympathizing Jewish friends saw Mary run off, they followed her, thinking she was on her way to the tomb to weep there.
MSG.32: Mary came to where Jesus was waiting and fell at his feet, saying, "Master, if only you had been here, my brother would not have died."
MSG.33: When Jesus saw her sobbing and the Jews with her sobbing, a deep anger welled up within him.
MSG.34: He said, "Where did you put him?" "Master, come and see," they said.
MSG.36: The Jews said, "Look how deeply he loved him."
MSG.37: Others among them said, "Well, if he loved him so much, why didn't he do something to keep him from dying? After all, he opened the eyes of a blind man."
MSG.38: Then Jesus, the anger again welling up within him, arrived at the tomb. It was a simple cave in the hillside with a slab of stone laid against it.
MSG.39: Jesus said, "Remove the stone." The sister of the dead man, Martha, said, "Master, by this time there's a stench. He's been dead four days!"
MSG.40: Jesus looked her in the eye. "Didn't I tell you that if you believed, you would see the glory of God?"
MSG.41: Then, to the others, "Go ahead, take away the stone." They removed the stone. Jesus raised his eyes to heaven and prayed, "Father, I'm grateful that you have listened to me.
MSG.42: I know you always do listen, but on account of this crowd standing here I've spoken so that they might believe that you sent me."
MSG.44: And he came out, a cadaver, wrapped from head to toe, and with a kerchief over his face. Jesus told them, "Unwrap him and let him loose."
MSG.45: That was a turnaround for many of the Jews who were with Mary. They saw what Jesus did, and believed in him.
MSG.46: But some went back to the Pharisees and told on Jesus.
MSG.47: The high priests and Pharisees called a meeting of the Jewish ruling body. "What do we do now?" they asked. "This man keeps on doing things, creating God-signs.
MSG.48: If we let him go on, pretty soon everyone will be believing in him and the Romans will come and remove what little power and privilege we still have."
MSG.49: Then one of them—it was Caiaphas, the designated Chief Priest that year—spoke up, "Don't you know anything?
MSG.50: Can't you see that it's to our advantage that one man dies for the people rather than the whole nation be destroyed?"
MSG.51: He didn't say this of his own accord, but as Chief Priest that year he unwittingly prophesied that Jesus was about to die sacrificially for the nation,
MSG.52: and not only for the nation but so that all God's exile-scattered children might be gathered together into one people.
MSG.53: From that day on, they plotted to kill him.
MSG.54: So Jesus no longer went out in public among the Jews. He withdrew into the country bordering the desert to a town called Ephraim and secluded himself there with his disciples.
MSG.55: The Jewish Passover was coming up. Crowds of people were making their way from the country up to Jerusalem to get themselves ready for the Feast.
MSG.56: They were curious about Jesus. There was a lot of talk of him among those standing around in the Temple: "What do you think? Do you think he'll show up at the Feast or not?"
MSG.57: Meanwhile, the high priests and Pharisees gave out the word that anyone getting wind of him should inform them. They were all set to arrest him.

NIV.1:
The Death of Lazarus
Now a man named Lazarus was sick. He was from Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha.
NIV.2: This Mary, whose brother Lazarus now lay sick, was the same one who poured perfume on the Lord and wiped his feet with her hair.
NIV.3: So the sisters sent word to Jesus, “Lord, the one you love is sick.”
NIV.4: When he heard this, Jesus said, “This sickness will not end in death. No, it is for God’s glory so that God’s Son may be glorified through it.”
NIV.5: Jesus loved Martha and her sister and Lazarus.
NIV.6: Yet when he heard that Lazarus was sick, he stayed where he was two more days.
NIV.7: Then he said to his disciples, “Let us go back to Judea.”
NIV.8: “But Rabbi,” they said, “a short while ago the Jews tried to stone you, and yet you are going back there?”
NIV.9: Jesus answered, “Are there not twelve hours of daylight? A man who walks by day will not stumble, for he sees by this world’s light.
NIV.10: It is when he walks by night that he stumbles, for he has no light.”
NIV.11: After he had said this, he went on to tell them, “Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep; but I am going there to wake him up.”
NIV.12: His disciples replied, “Lord, if he sleeps, he will get better.”
NIV.13: Jesus had been speaking of his death, but his disciples thought he meant natural sleep.
NIV.14: So then he told them plainly, “Lazarus is dead,
NIV.15: and for your sake I am glad I was not there, so that you may believe. But let us go to him.”
NIV.16: Then Thomas (called Didymus) said to the rest of the disciples, “Let us also go, that we may die with him.”
NIV.17:
Jesus Comforts the Sisters
On his arrival, Jesus found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb for four days.
NIV.18: Bethany was less than two miles from Jerusalem,
NIV.19: and many Jews had come to Martha and Mary to comfort them in the loss of their brother.
NIV.20: When Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went out to meet him, but Mary stayed at home.
NIV.21: “Lord,” Martha said to Jesus, “if you had been here, my brother would not have died.
NIV.22: But I know that even now God will give you whatever you ask.”
NIV.23: Jesus said to her, “Your brother will rise again.”
NIV.24: Martha answered, “I know he will rise again in the resurrection at the last day.”
NIV.25: Jesus said to her, “I am the resurrection and the life. He who believes in me will live, even though he dies;
NIV.26: and whoever lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this?”
NIV.27: “Yes, Lord,” she told him, “I believe that you are the Christ, the Son of God, who was to come into the world.”

Jesus, once more deeply moved, came to the tomb. It was a cave with a stone laid across the entrance.

“Take away the stone,” he said.

The Plot to Kill Jesus
Therefore many of the Jews who had come to visit Mary, and had seen what Jesus did, put their faith in him.

NET.1: Now a certain man named Lazarus was sick. He was from Bethany, the village where Mary and her sister Martha lived.
